mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS over the prior month signals easier financial conditions and a lower discount rate for equities, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when inflation is not accelerating sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not higher than baseline after month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal reverses when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates labor-market deterioration and weaker household income prospects, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through lower expected demand and higher recession odds.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE jumps are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in most cases, or if the sign is not negative after controlling for contemporaneous PAYEMS weakness.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both improve together, the signal indicates rising consumer spending supported by payroll gains, which should translate into stronger equity returns over the next 20 trading days via better revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS and PAYEMS strength does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal has no positive edge in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in INDPRO indicates an industrial production shock and broad real-activity loss, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 1.0% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if large monthly INDPRO declines are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST reflects improved housing demand and downstream activity, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through stronger cyclical growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if upward HOUST trends do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign is neutral/negative across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, policy stays restrictive in real terms and equity multiples should compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.3% month over month while FEDFUNDS stays above 3%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-plus-tight-policy triggers are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the combined condition fails to outperform CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDPC1 indicates a broad aggregate-demand shock that should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions spread across sectors.
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 growth is not associated with negative 60-day SPX returns in subsequent quarters, or if the predictive edge disappears after publication-lag alignment.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, falling UNRATE paired with rising PAYEMS signals rapid labor-market repair and stronger future consumption, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls and PAYEMS rises for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive labor-market improvement episodes do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint condition has no incremental value over either labor series alone.
